name: temper-core

metadata.name: temper-core
metadata.summary: Runtime support for Python generated by Temper

documentation:
# Temper Core Library for Python

This is the Python implementation of the Temper language's runtime support. It
includes the contents of `temper_core/**`.

Essentially, when a Temper module is compiled, this is what implements various
Temper builtin types, functions, etc. that meet the Temper specification.

modules.name: temper_core.testing
documentation:
A very simple test runner to grab what we need and format as xml, so we don't
have to install anything extra for testing.

class_variables.name: temper_core.testing.Failure.kind
documentation: "error" | "failure"

modules.name: temper_core
documentation: Implementation of many of the connected methods in temper.

functions.name: temper_core.generic_not_eq
documentation:
Checks if two objects are not exactly equal, caring about the sign of zeros of
floats.

functions.name: temper_core.arith_dub_div
documentation:
Performs division on Float64 (python float); dub stands for "double", like
the c datatype.

functions.name: temper_core.arith_int_mod
documentation:
Performs remainder on ints, raising an error if divisor is zero.
arith_int_mod(5, 3) == 2
arith_int_mod(-5, -3) == 1
arith_int_mod(5, -3) == -1
arith_int_mod(-5, -3) == -2

functions.name: temper_core.int_to_float64
documentation: Connected methods

functions.name: temper_core.new_unbound_promise
documentation: Support for Temper `new PromiseBuilder()`.

functions.name: temper_core._step_async_coro
documentation:
Start/resume a coroutine that might use await.

When representing a possibly asynchronous coroutine as a generator, we
need to step that generator.

This happens in two cases:
- we launch the coroutine so we need to step it once
- a promise resolves that it was waiting on, so we need to send back the
  resolution (either a result or an exception) and start it running
Called to start a Temper coroutine when it is launched
via `async{...}` and when a promise it is awaiting resolves.

functions.name: temper_core.adapt_generator_factory
documentation:
Take a generator factory and make it produce generators that
know how to await.  The yielding function underlying the generator
gets a `do_await` argument, before its regular initialization argument
list.

Then it can await by doing `yield do_await(some_future)`.
The `yield` cedes control, and the `send` use in `_step_async_coro`
allows for communicating the future's result  back to the generator
body.
_agfARGS = TypeVarTuple("_agfARGS") # Future type syntax

functions.name: temper_core.async_launch
documentation:
Launch a coroutine, stepping it once in another thread so that
it does some work.  Subsequent stepping is in response to
promise resolution when it yields via its `do_await`.

functions.name: temper_core.await_safe_to_exit
documentation: Called by generated main methods to allow async tasks to complete.

functions.name: temper_core.complete_promise
documentation:
Resolves the promise with the given value, but, like Temper
PromiseBuilder, second and subsequent resolutions are no-ops.

functions.name: temper_core.break_promise
documentation:
Resolves the promise exceptionally, but, like Temper
PromiseBuilder, second and subsequent resolutions are no-ops.

functions.name: temper_core._utf8_size
documentation: Utility functions

classes.name: temper_core.TemperComparable
documentation: Base class for comparable temper objects.

classes.name: temper_core.LoggingConsole

methods.name: temper_core.LoggingConsole.log
documentation: Log a message to the console.
One class per file to log data to the console.

classes.name: temper_core.Pair
documentation:
A pair of a key and a value.
Often used as Map entries
